decks.notes: The Dimir Control deck focuses on using a combination of removal spells, counterspells, and card draw to control the game and outvalue the opponent. It aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan by removing threats and countering key spells, while also generating card advantage with cards like Mental Note and Deep Analysis. The deck can also win through efficient threats like Gurmag Angler and Murmuring Mystic. By efficiently controlling the game and maintaining card advantage, the Dimir Control deck aims to outlast the opponent and secure victory.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game using various gates to gain life and provide mana, while using removal spells like Lightning Bolt and Galvanic Blast to eliminate threats. The deck also focuses on card advantage with cards like Cryogen Relic and Experimental Synthesizer. Additionally, the deck utilizes flying creatures like Glint Hawk and Kor Skyfisher to apply pressure on opponents. Overall, the deck aims to outlast opponents and win through attrition and incremental advantage.

The main concept of the deck is to use artifacts efficiently and quickly to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive strategy. By utilizing cheap artifacts and cards that benefit from having artifacts in play, such as Myr Enforcer and Reckoner's Bargain, the deck aims to quickly build a strong board presence and apply pressure to the opponent. The inclusion of cards like Galvanic Blast and Krark-Clan Shaman also provides direct damage and disruption options to control the game and secure a win.

The main idea of the deck is to quickly ramp up mana with cards like Wild Growth and Utopia Sprawl, allowing for early plays of powerful creatures such as Nyxborn Hydra and Avenging Hunter. The deck wants to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures like Eldrazi Repurposer and Bannerhide Krushok, while also having removal options like Ram Through and Malevolent Rumble to deal with threats. Writhing Chrysalis provides a way to create more creatures, while Arbor Elf helps with additional mana acceleration. The overall strategy is to apply pressure early and maintain board presence to secure victory.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game through removal spells and disruption while generating incremental advantage through sacrificing creatures and artifacts. By utilizing cards like Fanatical Offering and Eviscerator's Insight, the deck aims to generate value and maintain board presence. The inclusion of cards like Crypt Rats and Defile helps in wiping out opponent's creatures. Additionally, the deck has a land package that includes Golgari Rot Farm and Khalni Garden to ensure mana stability and provide a steady flow of resources.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize artifacts to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive strategies. By utilizing cards like Refurbished Familiar and Myr Enforcer, along with artifact lands like Great Furnace and Vault of Whispers, the deck aims to play creatures quickly and efficiently. Cards like Galvanic Blast and Krark-Clan Shaman provide additional reach and removal options, while cards like Ichor Wellspring and Thoughtcast help maintain card advantage. Overall, the deck focuses on utilizing artifacts to gain a tempo advantage and close out games quickly.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly deal damage to the opponent using aggressive creatures and burn spells. The goal is to maintain pressure on the opponent by consistently attacking and using direct damage spells, such as Lightning Bolt and Fireblast, to finish them off. The deck also includes cards like Faithless Looting and Grab the Prize to provide card advantage and ensure the player has the necessary resources to close out the game. Overall, this deck is designed to win games quickly through a combination of creature aggression and burn spells.

The main idea of this deck is to quickly establish board presence and pressure the opponent with efficient creatures and tempo plays. By utilizing cheap spells to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy, the deck aims to advance its game plan and close out the match before the opponent can stabilize. The focus on card advantage and an aggressive game plan makes this Mono Blue Aggro deck a formidable force in the Pauper format, allowing players to overwhelm opponents with a mix of creature threats and disruptive spells.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with small, aggressive creatures while also dealing direct damage through burn spells. By utilizing creatures like Voldaren Epicure and Sneaky Snacker alongside burn spells like Fireblast and Lightning Bolt, the deck aims to quickly chip away at the opponent's life total and secure a victory. The inclusion of cards like Grab the Prize and Highway Robbery also provide additional value and resources to ensure a steady stream of damage output.

The main idea of the deck is to control the game by using a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and creatures with disruptive abilities. By utilizing cards like Counterspell, Cast Down, and Spellstutter Sprite, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy and prevent them from building a strong board presence. Additionally, cards like Ninja of the Deep Hours and Faerie Seer provide card advantage and flexibility in the game plan. Overall, the deck focuses on outlasting the opponent and slowly gaining an advantage until victory is achieved.

This Jund Aggro deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures and removal spells. By utilizing efficient removal like Cast Down and Galvanic Blast, along with disruptive cards like Nihil Spellbomb and Krark-Clan Shaman, the deck can control the board while pushing damage through with creatures like Refurbished Familiar and Sagu Wildling. The inclusion of cards like Cleansing Wildfire and Twisted Landscape allows for mana fixing and card draw, ensuring that the deck maintains its aggressive game plan throughout the match.

This deck focuses on quickly building up powerful creatures through the use of auras and enchantments. By attaching multiple enchantments to a single creature, such as Ethereal Armor and Armadillo Cloak, you can create a formidable threat that can quickly overwhelm your opponent. The deck also includes cards like Utopia Sprawl and Abundant Growth to help ramp up mana production and ensure you can play your auras early and often. With a combination of efficient creatures and powerful enchantments, this deck aims to win the game through aggressive and efficient plays.

The main focus of this deck is to establish control through card advantage, disruption, and removal spells. By utilizing cards like Contaminated Aquifer and Lórien Revealed for early game advantage, along with counterspells and removal like Snuff Out and Cast Down to maintain control, the deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver the opponent. Thought Scour and Mental Note help with card filtering and graveyard manipulation, while proactive threats like Murmuring Mystic and Gurmag Angler provide a win condition. The deck also includes a mix of card draw and reactive spells for versatility in different matchups.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly flood the board with efficient and cheap elf creatures, utilizing cards such as Quirion Ranger and Lead the Stampede to draw more elves and maintain card advantage. The Nyxborn Hydra and Timberwatch Elf provide powerful finishers for the deck, while Wellwisher helps to keep you alive against aggressive opponents. The goal is to overwhelm your opponent with a swarm of elves and use Priest of Titania's mana-producing ability to fuel your powerful spells and creatures.

This deck focuses on utilizing low-cost, efficient creatures and spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent. By utilizing cards that enable card draw and manipulation, the deck aims to control the board while maintaining aggression. The key strategy is to quickly establish a board presence with cheap creatures like Delver of Secrets and Murmuring Mystic, while disrupting the opponent's game plan with counterspells and card draw. The deck's strength lies in its ability to consistently apply pressure and adapt to the opponent's plays.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game through a combination of counterspells, removal, and card advantage. By utilizing cards like Spellstutter Sprite, Counterspell, and Snuff Out, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and maintain control of the board. Faerie Seer and Augur of Bolas provide card selection and help set up powerful plays. The inclusion of Ninja of the Deep Hours allows for additional card draw and tempo advantage. Overall, this Dimir Control deck excels at slowing down the opponent and outlasting them through efficient removal and counter magic.
